creative_brief: Clean, minimal design. Hero image: a single product (e.g. pork belly from Bundarra) with a handwritten-style farm name label visible. Warm neutral palette. No stock photography — use real farm or hub imagery. CTA button in earthy green.
subject: The day I stopped buying groceries I couldn't trace
preheader: I stood in the aisle holding chicken, and I couldn't name a single thing about where it came from.
body:
I was standing in a supermarket aisle last winter, holding a tray of chicken breast marked "farm fresh."

I turned it over. No farm name. No region. No story. Just a barcode and a use-by date. I'd been buying this way for years — feeding it to my family without a second thought.

That evening I looked it up. The average grocery item in Australia travels over 2,400km before it reaches a shelf. It passes through warehouses, wholesalers, distribution centres. By the time you pick it up, the farmer who grew it has already lost most of the margin.

I couldn't unsee it.

That moment is the reason FarmThru exists. We built a farm-direct grocery store — no warehouses, no wholesalers, no middlemen — where every product traces back to a real farm you can look up by name.

Farms like Bundarra, where the pork belly comes from one property. Collins Wild Caught, pulling salmon and seasonal seafood off the South Coast of NSW. Paris Creek Farms in South Australia, producing carbon-neutral biodynamic dairy. Farmer Brown, whose eggs come from a single NSW farm.

Around 15 to 20 partner farms across NSW and beyond. Every one of them chosen because we'd feed their food to our own families.

This isn't a subscription box. There's no lock-in, no membership fee. You order what you need online, when you need it, and collect from our Brookvale hub Monday to Friday. Or we can get it to you across Sydney, Central Coast, or Wollongong.

No commitment. Just real food from farms with names.

Right now, we're building something bigger — a grocery system where farmers are paid fairly and families actually know what they're eating. We want you to be part of it.

If you've ever stood in an aisle and felt that quiet unease — you already know why this matters.

Come see where your food comes from.

[See our farms and shop now]

Rachel & the FarmThru team

P.S. Every product page shows the farm, the farmer, and how it got to you. That's the whole point.
